title: Cell Line Block and Microarray Preparation
author: Rona Ortenberg
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6pnhdme
materials: Re-suspend cells with the smallest possible volume of cold PBS and transfer them into one well in a 96-wells U-shaped plate (Cellstar, Sigma-Aldrich).

steps.title: Maintain cell lines in proper medium...
steps.instruction: Maintain cell lines in proper medium at 37°C in a humidified atmosphere of 5% CO2 in air. Assess cell confluence. If confluence is 70-80%, proceed.

steps.title: Harvest adherent cells with trypsin solution or cell dissociation solution
steps.instruction: Harvest adherent cells with trypsin solution or cell dissociation solution. Note: Harvesting cell with a cell scraper is not recommended, since it will lead to morphological damage of the cells or the death of a significant portion of the cells. Harvest cells growing in suspension using a mechanical force (pipettor).

steps.title: Count cells
steps.instruction: Count cells. The amount of cells per block should be addusted according the size of the cells. For example , take10-30 million of melanoma cells or 100-150 million of lymphocytes.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells for 5 minutes at 500 x g, 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells for 5 minutes at 500 x g, 4°C.

steps.title: Discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant. Re-suspended pellet with 15 ml cold PBS. Centrifuge cells again for 5 minutes at 515 x g, 4°C.

steps.title: Prepare fixation solution ( in a...
steps.instruction: Prepare fixation solution ( in a chemical hood) according to the next steps:

steps.title: Re-suspend pellet with a 15mL of...
steps.instruction: Re-suspend pellet with a 15mL of fixation solution and incubate for 5 minutes in room temperature (chemical hood).

steps.title: Centrifuge 5 minutes at 1250 x g, 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 minutes at 1250 x g, 4°C. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: The volume of each of the...
steps.instruction: The volume of each of the wells in such plate is ~280 microL. If further PBS volume is needed to transfer the cells into the well, centrifuge the plate for 5 minutes at 515 x g, 4°C and then discard the supernatant, hence making space for additional cells.

steps.title: Once all cells were transferred into...
steps.instruction: Once all cells were transferred into the well, centrifuge the plate for 5 minutes at 515 x g, 4°C and then discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Mix the pellet with 100-200 microL of the melted 0
steps.instruction: Mix the pellet with 100-200 microL of the melted 0.5% agarose gel according to the next steps:

steps.title: Transfer the agar-cell pellet to the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the agar-cell pellet to the appropriate tissue embedding cassette designated for cell block as described below:

steps.title: Embed in paraffin using standard conditions for surgical biopsies
steps.instruction: Embed in paraffin using standard conditions for surgical biopsies. This phase could be done by the pathological institution and usually takes 48 hours.

steps.title: Cut two 4 micron-thick slices
steps.instruction: Cut two 4 micron-thick slices: one for regular H&E stain and one for the specific stain.

steps.title: Stain the H&E slide
steps.instruction: Stain the H&E slide. The cells should be spread homogenously in the block.

steps.title: Use the Tissue Microarrayer (Beecher Instruments) and a 2-mm-gauge needle
steps.instruction: Use the Tissue Microarrayer (Beecher Instruments) and a 2-mm-gauge needle.

steps.title: Drill 3 cores from each donor...
steps.instruction: Drill 3 cores from each donor cell block to the recipient cell-line microarray (CMA) block.

steps.title: When the CMA is completed, heat...
steps.instruction: When the CMA is completed, heat it to 37°C in the designated oven to incorporate the cores into the wax.

steps.title: Stain every 40 th CMA section...
steps.instruction: Stain every 40 th CMA section for H&E to make sure that all cores are in place.

steps.title: Take 25mL of 4% PFA
steps.instruction: Take 25mL of 4% PFA

steps.title: Add 24mL of 100% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 24mL of 100% ethanol

steps.title: Add 1 mL of distillated water
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of distillated water

steps.title: Mix well
steps.instruction: Mix well

steps.title: Draw the necessary agar gel volume...
steps.instruction: Draw the necessary agar gel volume directly from the melted agar in the warmed bath.

steps.title: Quickly re-suspend cell pellet using the...
steps.instruction: Quickly re-suspend cell pellet using the agar and transfer the new suspension into a new flat-bottom 96-wells plate. The flat-bottom is needed creating a unified cell block structure.

steps.title: Cool the new 96-wells plate in 4°C for 45 minutes
steps.instruction: Cool the new 96-wells plate in 4°C for 45 minutes.

steps.title: Use a sterile scalpel (preferably a...
steps.instruction: Use a sterile scalpel (preferably a straight tip scalpel, as in scalpel #15). Gently insert the scalpel to the small space between the solid agarose-cells structure and the well's wall. Apply a very gentle force sideways. The entire solid mix should pop-out of the well. Note: if you did not stir properly while injecting the melted agar, a portion of the cell will be stuck at the bottom of the well and will be lost. A properly stir mix will leave no trace at the well.

steps.title: Put the solid cells-agarose structure in...
steps.instruction: Put the solid cells-agarose structure in a cassette, designated for cell blocks. The cassette can be for several hours in a tank full of 4% formaldehyde before final wourkup.
